tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No. The provided spec and context files were accurate and internally consistent — the Widget entity had a `name` field across `schema.ts`, `seed.ts`, and all widget spec scenarios, which I successfully removed. The only friction was a protocol parsing quirk with the `<<<END>>>` delimiter on `schema.ts` replacements, not an inaccuracy in the workspace files themselves.
tasks.nl_request: Drop the name detail from Widget — it's no longer collected.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No, nothing was inaccurate. The workspace state was consistent: `schema.ts` had `errorEnvelopeStyle: "code_message"`, the specs asserted `error.code`/`error.message`, and `server.ts` already contained the branching logic for both envelope styles — so the only implementation change needed was flipping the config value to `"type_detail"`.
tasks.nl_request: A cross-cutting API convention changed company-wide; apply the update here too.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: No, nothing was materially inaccurate. The workspace files (README grammar, existing specs, server.ts data-driven dispatch, schema/registry/seed structure) were all consistent with each other and with the PM brief. The only minor nit is the "GENERATED — do not hand-edit" boilerplate in schema.ts/registry.ts/seed.ts, since the workflow requires editing them directly, but that's a standard scaffold artifact rather than an actual inaccuracy.
tasks.nl_request: Product wants a Supplier list added so the team can start recording them.
